description_long: This town hall is a two-story stone and timber structure with a prominent clock or bell tower rising above the main building. The facade features a steeply pitched, shingled roof with dormer windows and a large circular window beneath the tower. The exterior walls are constructed from rough-hewn stone and accented with exposed wooden beams in a half-timbered style. The main entrance is a wooden double door set within a gabled porch, flanked by barrels and crates, suggesting a bustling civic hub. The tower is open at the top with a wooden balustrade and a conical roof, providing a lookout or bell-ringing platform. Decorative green-framed windows and cross-braced timberwork add to the medieval European architectural charm.
description: A medieval-style stone and timber town hall with a tall clock tower, steep shingled roofs, and decorative green windows. The main entrance is gabled and flanked by barrels and crates, with exposed wooden beams and a circular window beneath the tower.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance with a gabled porch, double wooden doors, and several barrels and crates. The tower and circular window are prominent, along with green-framed windows and detailed timberwork.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the stone tower with its open belfry and conical roof, as well as the shingled roof and timber-framed wall. The entrance is not visible from this angle.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the long stone and timber wall, the base of the tower, and the overhanging shingled roof. No doors or windows are visible on this side.
description_view_3: This end view shows the gabled side of the building, cross-braced timberwork, and the side of the main entrance. Barrels and crates are visible near the entrance.
attribute_facade_ornamentation: Exposed timber framing, cross-bracing, circular window, decorative green-framed windows, and a gabled porch
attribute_entrance_design: Single main entrance with double wooden doors, gabled porch, timber framing, and flanked by barrels and crates
